prompt: Telemetry corrections arriving out of order are moving measurements into the wrong hourly bucket, and two sites that reuse a vendor event identifier can collapse into one reading. A late replay of an older correction can also alter event metadata while leaving the newer value in place. Fresh readings whose observation and receipt times match roll up correctly. Restore idempotent ingestion and event-time aggregation.

repo_files.sql/telemetry_schema.sql:
CREATE TABLE readings (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    site_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    sensor_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    event_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    observed_at INTEGER NOT NULL,
    received_at INTEGER NOT NULL,
    value REAL NOT NULL,
    UNIQUE(sensor_id, event_id)
);
repo_files.sql/upsert_reading.sql:
INSERT INTO readings (
    site_id, sensor_id, event_id, observed_at, received_at, value
) VALUES (
    :site_id, :sensor_id, :event_id, :observed_at, :received_at, :value
)
ON CONFLICT(sensor_id, event_id) DO UPDATE SET
    observed_at = excluded.observed_at,
    received_at = MAX(readings.received_at, excluded.received_at),
    value = CASE
        WHEN excluded.received_at >= readings.received_at THEN excluded.value
        ELSE readings.value
    END;
repo_files.sql/hourly_rollup.sql:
SELECT
    site_id,
    strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:00:00Z', received_at, 'unixepoch') AS hour,
    ROUND(AVG(value), 2) AS average_value,
    COUNT(*) AS sample_count
FROM readings
WHERE received_at >= :window_start
  AND received_at < :window_end
GROUP BY site_id, hour
ORDER BY site_id, hour;
repo_files.tests/test_public.py:
import sqlite3
from pathlib import Path


ROOT = Path(__file__).parents[1]


def sql(name):
    return (ROOT / "sql" / name).read_text(encoding="utf-8")


def test_fresh_reading_rolls_up_in_its_hour():
    db = sqlite3.connect(":memory:")
    db.executescript(sql("telemetry_schema.sql"))
    db.execute(
        sql("upsert_reading.sql"),
        {
            "site_id": "public-site",
            "sensor_id": "temp",
            "event_id": "evt-1",
            "observed_at": 120,
            "received_at": 120,
            "value": 18.5,
        },
    )
    rows = db.execute(
        sql("hourly_rollup.sql"),
        {"window_start": 0, "window_end": 3600},
    ).fetchall()

    assert rows == [("public-site", "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z", 18.5, 1)]
